title: Validate MongoDB Projection with Nested Fields and Optional Keys

statement.markdown: Write a function `validateProjection` that takes a MongoDB projection object as input and returns `true` if the projection is valid, `false` otherwise. A valid projection is one where all nested fields have a valid key and the fields are not missing. Note that the projection may contain optional keys, which are denoted by a `$` symbol at the beginning of the key. For example, `{$field1: 1}` is a valid projection, while `{field1: 1, field2: {field3: 1}}` is not because `field3` is missing. You may assume that the input projection is a nested object.
statement.inputFormat: A MongoDB projection object as a string
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the projection is valid or not
statement.notes: The function should handle projections with nested fields and optional keys. Time complexity: O(n), where n is the number of keys in the projection. Space complexity: O(n), where n is the number of keys in the projection.

constraints.description: The number of keys in the projection

examples.explanation: The projection is valid because all fields are present.
The projection is valid because the optional key $street is present.
The projection is invalid because the field city is missing.

hints.text: Start by checking if the projection is a nested object.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: To validate a MongoDB projection, we need to check if all nested fields have a valid key and the fields are not missing. We can use a recursive function to check the projection object.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a brute-force approach by checking each field in the projection object. If a field is missing, we return false.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a recursive approach to check the projection object. If a field is missing, we return false.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a recursive approach to check the projection object. If a field is missing, we return false. We can also use a memoization technique to optimize the function.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function is correct because it checks all fields in the projection object and returns false if any field is missing.
editorial.dryRun: Example run: input: {"_id": 1, "name": "John", "address": {"street": "123 Main St"}}; output: true
